abstract:
Traffic forecasting is a fundamental challenge in multivariate time series analysis and a focal point of AI research. A significant hurdle in this domain is capturing the complex spatio-temporal heterogeneity and non-stationarity inherent in traffic streams. To address these issues, we introduce Spatio-Temporal Meta-Graph Learning, a novel Graph Structure Learning (GSL) mechanism designed specifically for spatio-temporal data. 

We implement this framework into the Meta-Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (MegaCRN). This model functions by integrating a Meta-Graph Learner—powered by a "Meta-Node Bank" that stores learned prototypes of traffic patterns—directly into a GCRN encoder-decoder architecture. We evaluated MegaCRN on two standard benchmarks (METR-LA and PEMS-BAY) and introduced a new, large-scale dataset, EXPY-TKY, which tracks traffic speeds across 1,843 expressway links in Tokyo. 

Experimental results show that MegaCRN consistently outperforms state-of-the-art models across all datasets. Furthermore, qualitative analysis reveals that our model successfully disentangles distinct spatial and temporal patterns, demonstrating robust adaptability to both routine traffic and anomalous events like accidents. Our code and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/deepkashiwa20/MegaCRN.
